behavior_summary: When the player 'looks at' or examines the FIREGLOB object, the object's U8P_FIREGLOB::look process displays a single short descriptive bark: 'fire globe'.
After emitting the bark the process suspends/ends; there is no branching, no menu, no flag reads or writes, and no external calls.
This script functions solely as a minimal U8P_FIREGLOB::look/inspect handler that provides a one-line description to the player.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FIREGLOB::look is executed as a process (processType 0x0208) triggered by the engine's U8P_FIREGLOB::look/examine action.
Control flow is linear: present a Bark ('fire globe') and then suspend/end the process.
There are Unknown/decompiler remnants (e.g., an opening '{' and 'suspend' marker) but they do not represent additional gameplay branching.
open_questions: Does the 'suspend' node represent a transient yield (allowing other processes to resume later) or a hard termination of the U8P_FIREGLOB::look process? (inferred but not explicit)
Is the bark text localized or keyed elsewhere in data files (the script shows the literal 'fire globe' but localization lookup is not present in the snippet)?

main_function.name: U8P_FIREGLOB::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the player's short description when examining the FIREGLOB object by emitting the text 'fire globe' and then suspending/ending the process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the game's U8P_FIREGLOB::look/examine action for the FIREGLOB (processType 0x0208).
Triggered when the player inspects or targets the FIREGLOB in the game world.

function_map.name: U8P_FIREGLOB::look
function_map.role: short description / U8P_FIREGLOB::look handler that barks a one-line description and suspends

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the player 'looks at' or examines the FIREGLOB object, the object's U8P_FIREGLOB::look process displays a single short descriptive bark: 'fire globe'.
